behaviors.description: All direct and transitive dependencies are extracted from the manifest and included in the vulnerability check.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is module handoff (manifest parser/resolver -> vulnerability-check input), not one isolated function. REAL-INFRA is not required: fixture manifests/lockfiles plus an in-process fake vulnerability client can reproduce missing transitive deps. Rejected System because behavior text does not require real network/OS/subprocess; it only requires extraction and inclusion.
behaviors.test_description: Run scanner components end-to-end in-process against fixture manifests/lockfiles containing direct and transitive deps; assert the dependency set sent to the vuln-check stage includes every expected package/version.

behaviors.description: The vulnerability database query returns results that match the exact version ranges in the manifest, not just the package name.
behaviors.justification: This is API-client/query-construction + response-filtering behavior across modules. A fake DB endpoint/client can verify version-range parameters and filtering; no real OS/network semantics are required for this specific failure mode. Rejected System because behavior text focuses on exact version-range matching logic, not real HTTP/runtime conditions.
behaviors.test_description: Wire query builder + client + matcher against a deterministic fake vulnerability DB; use same package with multiple vulnerable ranges and assert only CVEs matching manifest version constraints are returned.

behaviors.description: The severity sorting places Critical above High above Medium above Low, with CVEs of the same severity sorted by CVSS score.
behaviors.justification: Single local ordering/comparator logic with fixed inputs; no module boundary or I/O is needed to reproduce failures.
behaviors.test_description: Call the sort function with mixed severities and tied severities with different CVSS scores; assert exact output order.

behaviors.description: The manifest modification changes only the affected dependency's version, leaving all other entries unchanged.
behaviors.justification: Real failure often appears at parser/updater/serializer boundary (unintended edits outside target entry). In-process fixtures can catch this without real OS/network. Rejected System because behavior text is about content integrity of manifest mutation, not OS-level file behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Run manifest read->update->write pipeline on fixture manifest(s); diff before/after and assert only the targeted dependency version changed, with all other keys/values preserved.

behaviors.description: The auto-generated pull request is created on the real repository with the correct single-dependency diff.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly requires "real repository" and PR creation, which crosses real external API/repo boundaries. Mocks can hide auth/permission/branch/diff integration failures.
behaviors.test_description: Execute scanner PR-creation path against a real test repository (or sandbox repo) and verify one PR is opened with a diff containing only the intended dependency bump.

behaviors.description: A major version bump is correctly identified as a breaking change based on semver comparison.
behaviors.justification: Semver comparison is deterministic local logic; one function/object boundary with no I/O catches the failure.
behaviors.test_description: Feed semver pairs into breaking-change detector (e.g., 1.x -> 2.x true, 1.2 -> 1.3 false) and assert expected booleans.

behaviors.description: When a breaking change is detected, PR creation is skipped and a review flag is surfaced in the scanner output.
behaviors.justification: This is orchestration/state handoff across modules (semver decision -> PR service suppression -> output flag). Can be reproduced with in-process wiring and a fake PR client. Rejected System because behavior text does not require validating real repo API behavior here; it asserts conditional orchestration when breakage is detected.
behaviors.test_description: Wire detection + orchestration + reporting modules with a bump marked breaking; assert PR client is not invoked and scanner output includes review-required flag.
